meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Freelancer’s Guide to Faster Shipping
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'can't debug code' wall. Master AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship projects faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at broken logs. Leverage AI agents like Claude Code to perform root-cause analysis and Antigravity for automated test generation. By integrating these tools into your workflow, you can isolate bugs in seconds, maintain clean production code, and reclaim billable hours previously lost to frustrating, manual troubleshooting sessions.
content.intro:
You’re three hours into a sprint, the client is expecting a demo by morning, and your terminal is bleeding red errors. The adrenaline has faded, replaced by the crushing realization that you’re stuck in a dependency loop you didn't create. Every manual fix feels like pulling a thread on a sweater that’s rapidly unraveling.

But what if you stopped fighting the code and started orchestrating it? The era of manual 'print-statement' debugging is over. By treating your IDE as an intelligent collaborator rather than a blank canvas, you can offload the cognitive load of debugging to LLMs that process stack traces in milliseconds. It’s time to move from frantic patching to surgical precision.

steps.title: Contextualizing the Failure
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope. Capture the specific stack trace and the user action that triggered it to provide the AI with a clear 'ground truth'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this stack trace and the associated file. Identify the exact line of failure and explain the logical breakdown of the state management leading to this error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/services/api.ts --explain 'why is this throwing a null pointer exception?'

steps.title: Automated Isolation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to create an isolated reproduction script. If the AI can't reproduce the bug in a vacuum, the issue is environmental.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal reproduction script that isolates this bug from the rest of the application dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a reproduction test file for the auth flow bug'

steps.title: Predictive Fix Generation
steps.description: Don't just fix the symptom. Ask the AI to evaluate the ripple effects of your proposed solution before applying it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a patch for this bug. Analyze potential side effects on existing API routes and performance metrics before providing the code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'refactor the state handler to prevent race conditions as discussed'

steps.title: Test-Driven Validation
steps.description: Ensure the fix holds. Generate unit tests that specifically target the edge case that caused the crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that covers this edge case to ensure the bug does not regress in future deployments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for the updated logic and run them'

steps.title: Post-Mortem Documentation
steps.description: Capture the 'why' for your client. Use the AI to generate professional, non-technical summaries of the fix for your project delivery logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize this debugging session for a client report. Focus on the root cause and the preventative measures implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./debug_log.md --write 'create a client-friendly summary of this fix'

pro_tips: Use 'Chain of Thought' prompting when debugging complex architectural issues; force the AI to explain the data flow before suggesting a code change.
Always set the AI's 'temperature' to 0 for debugging tasks to ensure deterministic, logical reasoning rather than creative guessing.
Map your project's custom hooks and state patterns in a system prompt file for Claude Code so it understands your unique codebase conventions.
When a bug is persistent, ask the AI to 'act as a Senior SRE' to look for memory leaks or concurrency issues rather than just syntax errors.
Use Antigravity to generate 'negative tests'—tests designed to fail—to verify your error handling logic is actually robust.
content.next_steps: Once your bug is resolved, run a full regression suite and update your 'AI Context' files to ensure the model remembers this fix for future development.
content.conversion_block: Stop burning billable hours on manual debugging and start delivering production-ready code with the power of AI at your fingertips. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
